content.fact: Blasters are common personal weapons, but legality, visibility, quality, and social response vary by world, faction, and security context.
content.injection: Do not treat every public space as permissive. Blaster carry, checkpoints, confiscation, and local law can matter.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Broad Star Wars Legends continuity.

content.fact: Armor in Star Wars varies heavily by role, quality, species, and faction; stormtrooper, clone, bounty hunter, and Mandalorian armor are not interchangeable.
content.injection: Use armor type deliberately. It can signal faction, protection level, training, culture, fear, or disguise, but should not all behave the same.

content.fact: Bacta is advanced medical treatment, but serious injuries still involve availability, time, pain, recovery, scarring, cost, or medical supervision.
content.injection: Use bacta as powerful medicine with limits. Do not erase every injury instantly unless the story deliberately chooses a soft recovery mode.

content.fact: Cybernetics can restore function or enhance bodies, but they carry pain, maintenance, social meaning, vulnerability, and identity consequences.
content.injection: When characters use prosthetics or cybernetics, include maintenance, calibration, discomfort, dependence, or psychological weight when scene-relevant.

entries.title: Carbonite Freezing Is Risky for Living Beings

content.fact: Carbonite can preserve a living prisoner, but it is risky, traumatic, disorienting, and not routine medical stasis.
content.injection: Treat carbonite freezing as hazardous and coercive. A revived person may suffer disorientation, blindness, weakness, or trauma. Use this as a background rule only; narrower active era/source cards should override it.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Rebel Alliance before Endor.
Keep retrieval topic/entity-gated; this wide-context card should not flood unrelated scenes.

content.fact: Jedi and Sith holocrons can preserve teachings, gatekeepers, tests, warnings, and dangerous knowledge rather than simple databases.
content.injection: Use holocrons as interactive, tradition-bound repositories. Access may require Force sensitivity, alignment, trust, skill, or solving a gatekeeper’s challenge.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Broad Star Wars Legends continuity.

content.fact: Cloning exists in Star Wars Legends, but clone armies, Spaarti cylinders, Emperor clones, and local cloning projects belong to specific sources and eras.
content.injection: Do not introduce cloning as a generic solution to every death or army problem. Require era/source support for the cloning method in use.

content.fact: The Death Star is not a generic reusable weapon available across all eras; each Death Star project belongs to specific Imperial-era Context.
content.injection: Only use Death Star-level battle stations when the active era supports them. Do not make planet-killing superweapons casual background technology.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Imperial rule before Endor.
Respect story-position bounds from Episode III: Revenge of the Sith to Episode VI: Return of the Jedi.

entries.title: Sabacc and Credits Ground Everyday Scenes

content.fact: Credits, gambling, debts, cargo, docking fees, bribes, and games like sabacc ground many underworld and travel scenes.
content.injection: Use practical economy details when useful: credits, bribes, debts, repairs, docking fees, cargo value, gambling stakes, and unpaid favors.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Broad Star Wars Legends continuity.
Keep retrieval topic/entity-gated; this wide-context card should not flood unrelated scenes.

content.fact: Technical scans produce readings that must be interpreted, may be jammed, and can be fooled by masking, distance, or false signatures.
content.injection: Do not let scanners provide perfect truth. Treat readings as evidence that can be incomplete, spoofed, or misunderstood.
